name: Zuplo API Gateway
what_it_does: Provides a fully managed, OpenAPI-native API gateway with programmable request/response handlers, rate limiting, API key management, and an auto-generated developer portal deployed at the edge.
use_cases: Deploy an agent-facing API gateway in front of backend services in minutes using an OpenAPI spec without managing infrastructure
Issue and revoke API keys for agent consumers programmatically via the Zuplo management API with per-key rate limits
Add custom TypeScript handler logic at the edge to transform requests or inject auth headers before they reach agent backends
Auto-generate a developer portal from OpenAPI spec so other agents or developers can discover and test your API
Enforce per-consumer rate limits and quotas at the gateway layer without modifying backend agent code
not_for: Self-hosted or on-premises API gateway deployments where you own the infrastructure (use Kong or Tyk)
Complex service mesh traffic management between internal microservices (use Istio or Envoy)
Realtime WebSocket pub/sub messaging (use Ably or Pusher)
best_when: You want a managed, code-friendly API gateway that deploys from an OpenAPI spec with minimal DevOps overhead and includes a built-in developer portal.
avoid_when: You need self-hosted deployment, multi-region active-active routing with custom SLAs, or an open-source solution with no vendor lock-in.

auth.notes: Supports API key auth, JWT validation, and OAuth2 with multiple identity providers. API keys are managed as first-class resources with metadata and per-key rate limit overrides. Gateway-level auth is configured via policy YAML per route.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1 project, up to 1 million requests/month, community support
pricing.paid_tiers: Developer ~$10/mo, Team ~$59/mo, Business ~$299/mo, Enterprise custom; scales on request volume, projects, and SLA
pricing.notes: Free tier supports one project — suitable for a single agent API. Paid tiers unlock multiple projects, higher request volumes, and SLA guarantees.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limiting is configurable per consumer and per route; platform has no published hard cap on requests — subject to plan quotas

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Gateway returns structured error responses configurable by the developer. Default errors include HTTP status codes and JSON messages. Custom error shapes can be implemented in TypeScript handlers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Management API does not document idempotency keys. Route configuration is declarative and PUT-based (idempotent by nature). API key creation should use explicit IDs to be safe.
known_agent_gotchas: Gateway logic is written in TypeScript/JavaScript — agents generating gateway handler code must produce valid TypeScript that passes Zuplo's deployment validation
Configuration changes (routes, policies) are deployed as a new version; there is no hot-reload — agents must account for deployment latency when making gateway changes
API key metadata is stored as arbitrary JSON but has a size limit; agents storing rich per-key context must keep payloads small
The free tier limits to one project; agents managing multiple environments (dev/staging/prod) need a paid plan
Custom domains require DNS verification; agents automating full gateway provisioning must handle DNS propagation delays

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys are hashed at rest. JWT validation supports JWKS endpoints. OAuth2 integration with major IdPs. All traffic terminates TLS at the edge. Secret environment variables available for handler code.
